    • Plot a horizontal bar chart using theme_gray()

Hands-on Exercise 1

Author

ZHANG Xiaoyu

Getting Started

Install and launching R packages

The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in this computer. If they are,then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)

Importing the data

exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
Rows: 324 Columns: 8
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (7): CLASS, NAME, GENDER, RACE, ENGLISH, MATHS, SCIENCE
dbl (1): S/N

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Plotting a simple bar chart

ggplot(data = exam_data, 
       aes(x = fct_infreq(RACE))) + 
  geom_bar()

R Graphics VS ggplot

R Graphics

exam_data$MATHS <- as.numeric(exam_data$MATHS)
Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
hist(exam_data$MATHS)

ggplot

ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=10, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="black", 
                 fill="grey") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores")
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Essential Grammatical Elements in ggplot2

Call the ggplot() function

ggplot(data=exam_data)

Adds the aesthetic element

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS))

Plot a bar chart using geom_bar()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar()

Plot a dot plot using geom_dotplot()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot(binwidth=2.5,         
               dotsize = 0.5) +      
  scale_y_continuous(NULL,           
                     breaks = NULL) 
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`stat_bindot()`).

Create a simple histogram using geom_histogram()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram() 
`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Modifying a geometric object by changing geom()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20,            
                 color="black",      
                 fill="light blue") 
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Modifying a geometric object by changing aes()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 color="grey30")
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Plot the distribution of Maths scores using geom-density()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_density()  
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_density()`).
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS, 
           colour = GENDER)) +
  geom_density()
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_density()`).

Plot boxplots using geom_boxplot()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS,       
           x= GENDER)) +    
  geom_boxplot() 
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).

Plot the distribution of Maths score by gender using geom_violin

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_violin()
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_ydensity()`).

Plot scatterplot using geom_point()

exam_data$ENGLISH <- as.numeric(exam_data$ENGLISH)
Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20))
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

Plot the data points on the boxplots by using both geom_boxplot() and geom_point()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +                    
  geom_point(position="jitter", 
             size = 0.5)        
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

Add mean values by using stat_summary()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(geom = "point",       
               fun = "mean",         
               colour ="red",        
               size=4)               
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_summary()`).
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(stat="summary",        
             fun="mean",           
             colour="red",          
             size=4)          
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_summary()`).

Adding a best fit curve on a scatterplot

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(size=0.5)
Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_smooth()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              linewidth=0.5)
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_smooth()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

Working with facet_wrap()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20) +
    facet_wrap(~ CLASS)
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Plot a trellis plot using facet_grid()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20) +
  facet_grid(~ CLASS) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 40))
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

Flip the horizontal bar chart into vertical bar chart by using coord_flip()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip()

Fix both the y-axis and x-axis range from 0-100

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100))
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_smooth()`).
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

Plot a horizontal bar chart using theme_gray()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_gray()
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_classic()
ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()
